title: Using formation aid to improve sheet formation
sources: General papermaking practice; synthesis from Dard Hunter, Old Papermaking (1923) and modern hand-papermaking documentation
notes: PEO (polyethylene oxide) is added to the vat to reduce fibre clumping and improve the even distribution of fibres across the mould. Covers: how much to use (a small quantity dramatically changes vat behaviour), the characteristic slippery feel of a correctly sized vat, and how to drain the vat if the PEO is too concentrated.
